name: Git Config Expansion

tasks.title: Add new config fields to GitConfig struct
tasks.description: Add new config fields to GitConfig struct
tasks.body:
Add three new fields to the GitConfig struct in internal/config/types.go:
- CommitOnSuccess (bool, json: commit_on_success): controls whether the daemon commits after successful task completion. Default: true.
- CommitOnFailure (bool, json: commit_on_failure): controls whether the daemon commits partial work after task failure. Default: true.
- CommitState (bool, json: commit_state): controls whether .wolfcastle/ state is included in daemon commits. Default: true.

All three are subordinate to the existing AutoCommit master switch (ignored when auto_commit is false).

Also rename SkipHooksOnAutoCommit to SkipHooks (json: skip_hooks) per the spec, or confirm whether the existing field name should remain. Check whether a rename would break existing user configs and decide accordingly.

Update Defaults() in internal/config/config.go to set the new defaults.
Update Validate() in internal/config/validate.go if any validation is needed (the bool fields likely need no validation, but confirm CommitOnSuccess/CommitOnFailure aren't both false when auto_commit is true, which would be a no-op config worth warning about).

Reference the spec at .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-22T07-00Z-deterministic-git.md for the full config schema.

tasks.title: Add config field tests
tasks.body:
Write tests for the new config fields:
- Test that Defaults() returns the expected values for commit_on_success, commit_on_failure, and commit_state (all true).
- Test that a config with auto_commit: false ignores the fine-grained controls (this may be a behavioral test that belongs in the daemon commit logic tests, but the config layer should at least parse correctly).
- Test round-trip JSON marshaling of the new fields.
- Test that the renamed/new skip_hooks field (json: skip_hooks) deserializes correctly from user configs.

Look at existing config tests to follow the established patterns.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Git Config Expansion is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify new config fields deserialize correctly from JSON and that Defaults() returns expected values

breadcrumbs.text: Added CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState to GitConfig; renamed SkipHooksOnAutoCommit to SkipHooks; updated defaults, validation, iteration.go callsite, and config-reference docs
Added types_test.go with 9 tests for CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState, and SkipHooks: defaults, JSON round-trip, deserialization, Load parsing, and validation

Audit reviewed 5 source files: types.go, types_test.go, config.go, validate.go, iteration.go, plus config-reference.md. PASS. Build clean, all tests pass (including race detector), 95.7% coverage. No stale SkipHooksOnAutoCommit references remain. Three new fields (CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState) correctly declared, defaulted to true, validated, and tested with 9 tests. The fields are not yet consumed in daemon commit logic, which is correct: wiring is scoped to the daemon-commit-logic sibling node. One accepted tradeoff: the no-op validation (auto_commit true + both commit flags false) is an error rather than a warning, reasonable for now. No gaps, no escalations.

audit.result_summary: Added CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, and CommitState bool fields to GitConfig, each defaulting to true. Renamed SkipHooksOnAutoCommit to SkipHooks (default false). Updated Defaults(), ValidateStructure() (catches the no-op case where auto_commit is true but both commit flags are false), the single iteration.go callsite, and config-reference.md docs. Nine tests in types_test.go cover defaults, JSON round-trip, deserialization, Load integration, and validation. All tests pass with 95.7% coverage. The new fields are config-only at this stage; wiring them into the daemon commit path is scoped to the daemon-commit-logic sibling node.

task-0001.objective: Add CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, and CommitState fields to GitConfig and rename SkipHooksOnAutoCommit to SkipHooks
task-0001.what_happened: Added three new bool fields to GitConfig in types.go. Renamed SkipHooksOnAutoCommit to SkipHooks with json tag skip_hooks (no user configs reference the old tag, so the rename is safe). Updated Defaults() with new fields and changed SkipHooks default from true to false per spec. Added validation in ValidateStructure that catches the no-op case (auto_commit true, both commit_on_success and commit_on_failure false). Updated the single callsite in iteration.go and the config-reference.md docs.
went_well: Grep confirmed no user config files use skip_hooks_on_auto_commit, making the JSON tag rename safe
All 80+ config tests and all daemon tests pass with no changes needed
improvements: The validation for the no-op config is an error rather than a warning; a future task may want to make it a soft warning instead
action_items: The new fields are not yet consumed in daemon commit logic; the daemon-commit-logic node handles wiring them in

task-0002.objective: Write tests for the new GitConfig commit control fields added in task-0001
task-0002.what_happened: Created internal/config/types_test.go with 9 tests covering: Defaults() returning true for CommitOnSuccess/CommitOnFailure/CommitState, Defaults() returning false for SkipHooks, JSON round-trip marshaling of GitConfig, deserialization of skip_hooks from JSON, deserialization of all new fields from JSON, Load() parsing fine-grained fields when auto_commit is false, ValidateStructure catching the no-op config (auto_commit true with both commit flags false), ValidateStructure accepting auto_commit false with both flags false, and verification of JSON field name tags.
went_well: All tests passed on first run
Coverage at 95.7%, well above the 90% threshold
improvements: Could add fuzz testing for the JSON deserialization path, but table-driven tests are sufficient for these simple bool fields
action_items: None; the config test surface is complete for these fields
